us-politics.headline: Trump Brokers Iran Ceasefire Amid Internal GOP Tensions
us-politics.body:
President Trump has navigated a delicate diplomatic breakthrough with Iran, securing a fragile ceasefire after weeks of escalating tensions. The peace talks in Pakistan represent a potential turning point in US-Iran relations - though deep divisions remain both internationally and within Trump's own political base.

While some Republican supporters praise the diplomatic effort, others question the strategy. The negotiations have exposed fault lines in Trump's coalition, with foreign policy hawks and diplomatic moderates offering competing interpretations of the emerging agreement.

sources.title: Week in Politics: Trump navigates ceasefire with Iran; division among his supporters
Vance, who wasn’t keen on Iran war, now tasked with trying to end it
Pakistan hosts U.S.-Iran peace talks after weeks of frantic diplomacy
Vance arrives in Pakistan for high-stakes Iran talks as ‘fragile’ ceasefire teeters

us-politics.headline: Swalwell Sexual Assault Claims Spark California Democratic Infighting
us-politics.body:
California Governor candidate Eric Swalwell faces mounting pressure after sexual assault allegations from a former staffer emerged. Democratic leadership - including Nancy Pelosi - has swiftly condemned the accusations as indefensible, creating potential complications for Swalwell's gubernatorial campaign.

The scandal highlights ongoing tensions within the Democratic Party about accountability and candidate vetting. Party leaders are struggling to balance internal discipline with electoral strategy as the 2028 political landscape takes shape.

sources.title: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
Pelosi, California Dems slam Swalwell over bombshell sexual assault allegations: 'Indefensible'

us-politics.headline: Education Department Reshapes Title IX Gender Identity Guidelines
us-politics.body:
The Trump administration has rescinded previous Title IX agreements on gender identity, signaling a significant shift in federal education policy. The move creates uncertainty for schools and potentially rolls back protections for transgender students.

Education advocates and civil rights groups are preparing legal challenges, while conservative supporters view the change as a necessary correction to previous policies. The decision reflects broader cultural and political debates about gender, education, and federal intervention.

sources.title: Title IX agreements on shaky ground after Education Department rescinds gender-identity deals
Early audition for 2028 hopefuls focuses on civil rights issues

au-politics.headline: PM's Singapore Trip: Diplomatic Success or Missed Opportunity?
au-politics.body: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese returned from Singapore without securing a major diesel shipment - but diplomatic sources suggest the visit achieved broader strategic goals. The Guardian argues the trip was more nuanced than simple fuel procurement, highlighting potential long-term regional cooperation.

sources.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success

au-politics.headline: Universities Grapple with Free Speech and Gaza Conflict Tensions
au-politics.body: RMIT University dropped misconduct proceedings against a student who accused the institution of being complicit in Gaza-related violence. The case highlights growing tensions around campus free speech and international conflict discourse. Student activism continues to challenge institutional responses to complex geopolitical issues.

sources.title: RMIT drops misconduct case against student who accused university of being ‘complicit in Gaza genocide’

au-politics.headline: Police Powers Expansion Sparks Government Controversy
au-politics.body: The government is moving to permanently entrench expanded police powers, a decision drawing sharp criticism from civil liberties advocates. The proposed legislation would codify emergency-era regulations into standard legal frameworks. Critics argue the move represents an unprecedented expansion of state authority.

sources.title: Government set to make extreme police powers permanent

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Returns Safely from Historic Moon Mission
world.body: NASA celebrated a landmark achievement as the Artemis II mission successfully splashed down in the Pacific Ocean. Four astronauts completed humanity's first crewed lunar voyage in over 50 years - a critical step toward returning humans to the moon. Recovery teams quickly retrieved the crew from their capsule, marking a pivotal moment for the US space program. NASA officials praised the mission's success while acknowledging significant work remains to establish sustainable lunar exploration.

sources.title: Artemis II crew seen on recovery ship after moon mission return
NASA hails success of Artemis moon mission but says more work to do
Artemis II marks historic lunar return with Pacific splashdown success
Title: Artemis II astronauts journey back to Earth after Moon mission

world.headline: US-Iran Tensions Simmer as Critical Talks Approach
world.body: High-stakes diplomatic negotiations loom between the United States and Iran amid ongoing regional tensions. US and Iranian representatives are preparing for crucial talks aimed at de-escalating conflict and potentially reopening the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. Pakistan has positioned itself as a key intermediary - with its prime minister describing the upcoming discussions as potentially make-or-break for regional stability. The 43-day conflict has strained international relations and raised global economic concerns.

sources.title: Video: ‘Crucial talks’ says Al Jazeera reporter at US-Iran meeting venue
Video: Vance arrives in Pakistan for talks with Iran
Iran war: What is happening on day 43 of the US-Iran conflict?
Pakistan ambassador speaks to Al Jazeera on eve of US-Iran talks
Pakistan’s prime minister calls US-Iran talks ‘make or break’
Trump says Strait of Hormuz to reopen ‘soon’ as US, Iran head to talks

world.headline: Israel Continues Gaza Attacks Despite International Pressure
world.body: Israeli military operations in Gaza intensified, with at least seven Palestinians killed in recent drone and ground attacks. The conflict continues to draw international condemnation - particularly from regional leaders concerned about escalating violence. South Korea has publicly challenged Israel over Palestinian human rights abuses, adding diplomatic pressure. Israel remains defiant - rejecting ceasefire proposals with Hezbollah and maintaining its current military posture in the region.

sources.title: Israel in row with South Korean leader over Palestinian abuse concerns
At least seven Palestinians killed in Israeli attacks on Gaza
Israel rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah before Lebanon talks next week
Israeli drone attack kills Palestinians near Gaza mosque

ai.headline: NASA Completes Historic Artemis II Lunar Mission
ai.body:
Four astronauts returned safely to Earth after completing the Artemis II mission - a critical milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program. The spacecraft splashed down in the Pacific Ocean, marking the first crewed lunar orbit since the Apollo era.

The successful mission sets the stage for future lunar expeditions. NASA will now analyze mission data and prepare for subsequent Artemis missions, which aim to establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

sources.title: The Artemis II mission has ended. Where does NASA go from here?
Four astronauts are back home after a daring ride around the Moon
Artemis II safely splashes down

ai.headline: AI and Tech Face Mounting Legal and Security Challenges
ai.body:
A series of high-profile incidents highlight growing tensions in the tech world. Anthropic temporarily banned a user from accessing Claude - its AI model - while OpenAI CEO Sam Altman faced a serious security threat when a 20-year-old was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at his home.

Legal pressures are mounting across the industry. Californians have sued over an AI tool recording doctor visits, and the US government continues to demand user data from tech platforms, signaling intensifying scrutiny of AI and digital technologies.

sources.title: Californians sue over AI tool that records doctor visits
Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
Report: US demands Reddit unmask ICE critic, summons firm to grand jury
Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think

ai.headline: France Shifts Tech Strategy - Dumps Windows for Linux
ai.body:
The French government is moving away from US-based technology, announcing a strategic shift to Linux from Windows. Officials cited national security concerns as a primary motivation for the change.

This move signals growing geopolitical tensions in technology and underscores increasing skepticism about US tech dominance. The decision could prompt other governments to reassess their digital infrastructure and software dependencies.

sources.title: France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, says US tech a strategic risk
Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI

btc.headline: Bhutan Dumps Bitcoin, Signals Retreat from Crypto Strategy
btc.body:
Bhutan has dramatically scaled back its bitcoin experiment, selling 70% of its holdings in just 18 months. The small Himalayan nation - once touted as a crypto mining haven - appears to be walking away from its ambitious digital asset strategy.

The sell-off suggests growing skepticism about bitcoin's long-term potential in national investment portfolios. Bhutan's pivot could signal broader challenges for countries attempting to leverage cryptocurrency as a national economic tool.

sources.title: Bhutan has sold 70% of its bitcoin in 18 months. It may have stopped BTC mining too.

food.name: Kyoto Spring Risotto
food.description: Creamy arborio rice with fresh bamboo shoots, spring peas, and cherry blossoms, finished with yuzu zest
food.tip: Toast rice in butter before adding liquid to enhance nutty undertones

exercise.name: Urban Parkour Flow Session
exercise.description: Improvised movement through city landscape, combining precision jumps, wall runs, and fluid transitions between architectural elements

learn.description: Exploring how nature's evolutionary solutions can inspire innovative engineering and architectural approaches to solving complex environmental challenges
learn.why: Reveals profound interconnections between natural systems and human innovation, offering transformative perspectives on sustainable problem-solving
